url.description: The URL to access the project pages.

gitlab:index/projectProtectedEnvironment.description: The `gitlab.ProjectProtectedEnvironment` resource manages the lifecycle of a protected environment in a project. > In order to use a user or group in the `deployAccessLevelsAttribute` configuration, you need to make sure that users have access to the project and groups must have this project shared. You may use the `gitlab.ProjectMembership` and `gitlabProjectSharedGroup` resources to achieve this. Unfortunately, the GitLab API does not complain about users and groups without access to the project and just ignores those. In case this happens you will get perpetual state diffs. **Upstream API**: [GitLab REST API docs](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/protected_environments/) ## Import Starting in Terraform v1.5.0, you can use an import block to import `gitlab.ProjectProtectedEnvironment`. For example: Importing using the CLI is supported with the following syntax: GitLab protected environments can be imported using an id made up of `projectId:environmentName`, for example: ```sh $ pulumi import gitlab:index/projectProtectedEnvironment:ProjectProtectedEnvironment bar 123:production ```

gitlab:index/projectPushRules:ProjectPus.description: The `gitlab.ProjectPushRules` resource manages the lifecycle of push rules on a project. > This resource will compete with the `gitlab.Project` resource if push rules are also defined as part of that resource, since this resource will take over ownership of the project push rules created for the referenced project. It is recommended to define push rules using this resource OR in the `gitlab.Project` resource, but not in both as it may result in terraform identifying changes with every "plan" operation. > This resource requires a GitLab Enterprise instance with a Premium license to set the push rules on a project. **Upstream API**: [GitLab API docs](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/project_push_rules/) ## Example Usage ```typescript import * as pulumi from "@pulumi/pulumi"; import * as gitlab from "@pulumi/gitlab"; const sample = new gitlab.ProjectPushRules("sample", { project: "42", authorEmailRegex: "@gitlab.com$", branchNameRegex: "(feat|fix)\\/*", commitCommitterCheck: true, commitCommitterNameCheck: true, commitMessageNegativeRegex: "ssh\\:\\/\\/", commitMessageRegex: "(feat|fix):.*", denyDeleteTag: false, fileNameRegex: "(jar|exe)$", maxFileSize: 4, memberCheck: true, preventSecrets: true, rejectUnsignedCommits: false, }); ``` ```python import pulumi import pulumi_gitlab as gitlab sample = gitlab.ProjectPushRules("sample", project="42", author_email_regex="@gitlab.com$", branch_name_regex="(feat|fix)\\/*", commit_committer_check=True, commit_committer_name_check=True, commit_message_negative_regex="ssh\\:\\/\\/", commit_message_regex="(feat|fix):.*", deny_delete_tag=False, file_name_regex="(jar|exe)$", max_file_size=4, member_check=True, prevent_secrets=True, reject_unsigned_commits=False) ``` ```csharp using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using Pulumi; using GitLab = Pulumi.GitLab; return await Deployment.RunAsync(() => { var sample = new GitLab.ProjectPushRules("sample", new() { Project = "42", AuthorEmailRegex = "@gitlab.com$", BranchNameRegex = "(feat|fix)\\/*", CommitCommitterCheck = true, CommitCommitterNameCheck = true, CommitMessageNegativeRegex = "ssh\\:\\/\\/", CommitMessageRegex = "(feat|fix):.*", DenyDeleteTag = false, FileNameRegex = "(jar|exe)$", MaxFileSize = 4, MemberCheck = true, PreventSecrets = true, RejectUnsignedCommits = false, }); }); ``` ```go package main import ( "github.com/pulumi/pulumi-gitlab/sdk/v10/go/gitlab" "github.com/pulumi/pulumi/sdk/v3/go/pulumi" ) func main() { pulumi.Run(func(ctx *pulumi.Context) error { _, err := gitlab.NewProjectPushRules(ctx, "sample", &gitlab.ProjectPushRulesArgs{ Project: pulumi.String("42"), AuthorEmailRegex: pulumi.String("@gitlab.com$"), BranchNameRegex: pulumi.String("(feat|fix)\\/*"), CommitCommitterCheck: pulumi.Bool(true), CommitCommitterNameCheck: pulumi.Bool(true), CommitMessageNegativeRegex: pulumi.String("ssh\\:\\/\\/"), CommitMessageRegex: pulumi.String("(feat|fix):.*"), DenyDeleteTag: pulumi.Bool(false), FileNameRegex: pulumi.String("(jar|exe)$"), MaxFileSize: pulumi.Int(4), MemberCheck: pulumi.Bool(true), PreventSecrets: pulumi.Bool(true), RejectUnsignedCommits: pulumi.Bool(false), }) if err != nil { return err } return nil }) } ``` ```hcl pulumi { required_providers { gitlab = { source = "pulumi/gitlab" } } } resource "gitlab_projectpushrules" "sample" { project = 42 author_email_regex = "@gitlab.com$" branch_name_regex = "(feat|fix)\\/*" commit_committer_check = true commit_committer_name_check = true commit_message_negative_regex = "ssh\\:\\/\\/" commit_message_regex = "(feat|fix):.*" deny_delete_tag = false file_name_regex = "(jar|exe)$" max_file_size = 4 member_check = true prevent_secrets = true reject_unsigned_commits = false } ``` ```java package generated_program; import com.pulumi.Context; import com.pulumi.Pulumi; import com.pulumi.core.Output; import com.pulumi.gitlab.ProjectPushRules; import com.pulumi.gitlab.ProjectPushRulesArgs; import java.util.ArrayList; import java.util.Arrays; import java.util.Map; import java.io.File; import java.nio.file.Files; import java.nio.file.Paths; public class App { public static void main(String[] args) { Pulumi.run(App::stack); } public static void stack(Context ctx) { var sample = new ProjectPushRules("sample", ProjectPushRulesArgs.builder() .project("42") .authorEmailRegex("@gitlab.com$") .branchNameRegex("(feat|fix)\\/*") .commitCommitterCheck(true) .commitCommitterNameCheck(true) .commitMessageNegativeRegex("ssh\\:\\/\\/") .commitMessageRegex("(feat|fix):.*") .denyDeleteTag(false) .fileNameRegex("(jar|exe)$") .maxFileSize(4) .memberCheck(true) .preventSecrets(true) .rejectUnsignedCommits(false) .build()); } } ``` ```yaml resources: sample: type: gitlab:ProjectPushRules properties: project: 42 authorEmailRegex: '@gitlab.com$' branchNameRegex: (feat|fix)\/* commitCommitterCheck: true commitCommitterNameCheck: true commitMessageNegativeRegex: ssh\:\/\/ commitMessageRegex: (feat|fix):.* denyDeleteTag: false fileNameRegex: (jar|exe)$ maxFileSize: 4 memberCheck: true preventSecrets: true rejectUnsignedCommits: false ``` ## Import Starting in Terraform v1.5.0, you can use an import block to import `gitlab.ProjectPushRules`. For example: Importing using the CLI is supported with the following syntax: Gitlab project push rules can be imported with a key composed of ``, for example: ```sh $ pulumi import gitlab:index/projectPushRules:ProjectPushRules sample "42" ```

gitlab:index/topic:Topic.description: The `gitlab.Topic` resource manages the lifecycle of topics that are then assignable to projects. > Topics are the successors for project tags. Aside from avoiding terminology collisions with Git tags, they are more descriptive and better searchable. **Upstream API**: [GitLab REST API docs for topics](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/topics/) ## Import Starting in Terraform v1.5.0, you can use an import block to import `gitlab.Topic`. For example: Importing using the CLI is supported with the following syntax: The `id` must be an integer for the id of the topic you want to import, for example: ```sh $ pulumi import gitlab:index/topic:Topic functional_programming 1 ```

gitlab:index/userAvatar:UserAvatar.description: The `gitlab.UserAvatar` resource allows users to manage the lifecycle of a user avatar. The resource can also be used to set the avatar on project or group access tokens, as well as on service accounts. > The `token` attribute is optional only when the GitLab token used by the provider has an administrator scope, as this allows an administrator to manage user avatars. > The provided `token` must have the `api` scope in order to set the avatar. **Upstream API**: [GitLab API docs](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/users/#upload-an-avatar-for-yourself) ## Import Starting in Terraform v1.5.0, you can use an import block to import `gitlab.UserAvatar`. For example: Importing using the CLI is supported with the following syntax: A GitLab User Avatar can be imported using the user id, for example: ```sh $ pulumi import gitlab:index/userAvatar:UserAvatar example "12345" ``` NOTE: the `token` and `avatar` resource attributes are not available for imported resources as this information cannot be read from the GitLab API.

gitlab:index/userRunner:UserRunner.description: The `gitlab.UserRunner` resource allows creating a GitLab runner using the new [GitLab Runner Registration Flow](https://docs.gitlab.com/ci/runners/new_creation_workflow/). **Upstream API**: [GitLab REST API docs](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/users/#create-a-runner-linked-to-a-user) ## Example Usage ```typescript import * as pulumi from "@pulumi/pulumi"; import * as gitlab from "@pulumi/gitlab"; // Create a project runner const projectRunner = new gitlab.UserRunner("project_runner", { runnerType: "project_type", projectId: 123456, description: "A runner created using a user access token instead of a registration token", tagLists: [ "a-tag", "other-tag", ], untagged: true, }); // Create a group runner const groupRunner = new gitlab.UserRunner("group_runner", { runnerType: "group_type", groupId: 123456, }); // Create a instance runner const instanceRunner = new gitlab.UserRunner("instance_runner", {runnerType: "instance_type"}); const configToml = pulumi.interpolate`concurrent = 1 check_interval = 0 [session_server] session_timeout = 1800 [[runners]] name = \"my_gitlab_runner\" url = \"https://example.gitlab.com\" token = \"${groupRunner.token}\" executor = \"docker\" [runners.custom_build_dir] [runners.cache] [runners.cache.s3] [runners.cache.gcs] [runners.cache.azure] [runners.docker] tls_verify = false image = \"ubuntu\" privileged = true disable_entrypoint_overwrite = false oom_kill_disable = false disable_cache = false volumes = [\"/cache\", \"/certs/client\"] shm_size = 0 `; ``` ```python import pulumi import pulumi_gitlab as gitlab # Create a project runner project_runner = gitlab.UserRunner("project_runner", runner_type="project_type", project_id=123456, description="A runner created using a user access token instead of a registration token", tag_lists=[ "a-tag", "other-tag", ], untagged=True) # Create a group runner group_runner = gitlab.UserRunner("group_runner", runner_type="group_type", group_id=123456) # Create a instance runner instance_runner = gitlab.UserRunner("instance_runner", runner_type="instance_type") config_toml = group_runner.token.apply(lambda token: f"""concurrent = 1 check_interval = 0 [session_server] session_timeout = 1800 [[runners]] name = \"my_gitlab_runner\" url = \"https://example.gitlab.com\" token = \"{token}\" executor = \"docker\" [runners.custom_build_dir] [runners.cache] [runners.cache.s3] [runners.cache.gcs] [runners.cache.azure] [runners.docker] tls_verify = false image = \"ubuntu\" privileged = true disable_entrypoint_overwrite = false oom_kill_disable = false disable_cache = false volumes = [\"/cache\", \"/certs/client\"] shm_size = 0 """) defaultBranchProtection.description: Whether developers and maintainers can push to the applicable default branch. Use `defaultBranchProtectionDefaults` instead, to be removed in 19.0.

defaultBranchProtectionDefaults.description: Default protection settings applied to the default branch of new projects in this group.

duoAvailability.description: Indicates whether GitLab Duo features are enabled for the group. Valid values are `defaultOn`, `defaultOff`, `neverOn`.

duoFeaturesEnabled.description: Whether GitLab Duo features are enabled for the group.

emailsDisabled.deprecationMessage: Use `emailsEnabled` instead, to be removed in 19.0.
emailsDisabled.description: Whether email notifications are disabled for this group. Use `emailsEnabled` instead, to be removed in 19.0.

emailsEnabled.description: Whether email notifications are enabled for this group.

enabledGitAccessProtocol.description: Restrict access to group by allowing only certain protocols. Valid values are `ssh`, `http`, `all`.

experimentFeaturesEnabled.description: Whether experimental features are enabled for the group.

extraSharedRunnersMinutesLimit.description: Available in Self-Managed, Premium and Ultimate plans. Can be set by administrators only. Additional CI/CD minutes for this group.

fileTemplateProjectId.description: The ID of the project used to load custom file templates.

fullName.description: The full name of the group.

fullPath.description: The full path of the group.

groupId.description: The ID of the group.

id.description: The ID of this datasource. In the format `<group-id>`.

ipRestrictionRanges.description: Comma-separated list of IP addresses or subnet masks that restrict access to the group.

ldapAccess.description: Default access level for members synced from LDAP.

ldapCn.description: LDAP common name used to sync members from an LDAP group.

lfsEnabled.description: Boolean, is LFS enabled for projects in this group.

lockDuoFeaturesEnabled.description: Whether the GitLab Duo features setting is enforced for all subgroups.

lockMathRenderingLimitsEnabled.description: Whether the math rendering limits setting is enforced for all subgroups.

markedForDeletionOn.description: Date on which the group was marked for deletion.

mathRenderingLimitsEnabled.description: Whether math rendering limits are enabled for the group.

maxArtifactsSize.description: Maximum artifacts size for the group, in MB.

membershipLock.description: Users cannot be added to projects in this group.

mentionsDisabled.description: Whether mentions are disabled for this group.

onlyAllowMergeIfAllDiscussionsAreResolve.description: Default to only allowing merge if all discussions are resolved for new projects in the group.

onlyAllowMergeIfPipelineSucceeds.description: Default to only allowing merge if the pipeline succeeds for new projects in the group.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization this group belongs to.

parentId.description: Integer, ID of the parent group.

preventForkingOutsideGroup.description: When enabled, users can not fork projects from this group to external namespaces.

preventSharingGroupsOutsideHierarchy.description: When enabled, users cannot invite other groups outside of the top-level group's hierarchy. This option is only available for top-level groups.

projectCreationLevel.description: Determine which roles can create projects in the group. Possible values are `noone`, `maintainer`, `developer`, `owner`, `administrator`.

pushRules.description: Push rules for the group. Push rules are only available on Premium and Ultimate plans, and only if the authenticated user has permission to read them.

repositoryStorage.description: Repository storage shard the group's projects use. (admin only)

requestAccessEnabled.description: Boolean, is request for access enabled to the group.

requireTwoFactorAuthentication.description: Require all users in this group to set up two-factor authentication.

runnersToken.description: The group level registration token to use during runner setup.

shareWithGroupLock.description: Prevent sharing a project with another group within this group.

sharedRunnersMinutesLimit.description: Available in Self-Managed, Premium and Ultimate plans. Can be set by administrators only. Maximum number of monthly CI/CD minutes for this group. Can be nil (default; inherit system default), 0 (unlimited), or > 0.

sharedRunnersSetting.description: Enable or disable shared runners for a group's subgroups and projects. Valid values are: `enabled`, `disabledAndOverridable`, `disabledAndUnoverridable`, `disabledWithOverride`.

sharedWithGroups.description: Describes groups which have access shared to this group.

statistics.description: Statistics for the group. Keys: `commitCount`, `storageSize`, `repositorySize`, `wikiSize`, `lfsObjectsSize`, `jobArtifactsSize`, `pipelineArtifactsSize`, `packagesSize`, `snippetsSize`, `uploadsSize`, `containerRegistrySize`.

subgroupCreationLevel.description: Determine which roles can create subgroups in the group. Possible values are `owner`, `maintainer`.

twoFactorGracePeriod.description: Grace period, in hours, before enforcing two-factor authentication on group members.

visibilityLevel.description: Visibility level of the group. Possible values are `private`, `internal`, `public`.

webUrl.description: Web URL of the group.

wikiAccessLevel.description: The group's wiki access level. Only available on Premium and Ultimate plans. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

allowMergeOnSkippedPipeline.description: Whether `allowMergeOnSkippedPipeline` is enabled for the project.

allowPipelineTriggerApproveDeployment.description: Set whether or not a pipeline triggerer is allowed to approve deployments. Premium and Ultimate only.

analyticsAccessLevel.description: Set the analytics access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

approvalsBeforeMerge.deprecationMessage: Use the Merge Request Approvals API instead, to be removed in 20.0.
approvalsBeforeMerge.description: The number of approvals needed in a merge request.

archived.description: Whether the project is in read-only mode (archived).

autoCancelPendingPipelines.description: Auto-cancel pending pipelines. This isn't a boolean, but `enabled`/`disabled`.

autoDevopsDeployStrategy.description: Auto Deploy strategy. Valid values are `continuous`, `manual`, `timedIncremental`.

autoDevopsEnabled.description: Enable Auto DevOps for this project.

autoDuoCodeReviewEnabled.description: Whether GitLab Duo code review is enabled for the project.

autocloseReferencedIssues.description: Set whether auto-closing referenced issues on default branch.

avatarUrl.description: The avatar URL of the project.

buildCoverageRegex.description: Build coverage regex for the project.

buildGitStrategy.description: The Git strategy. Defaults to `fetch`.

buildTimeout.description: The maximum amount of time, in seconds, that a job can run.

buildsAccessLevel.description: Set the builds access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

canCreateMergeRequestIn.description: Whether the calling user can create merge requests in this project.

ciAllowForkPipelinesToRunInParentProject.description: Whether pipelines triggered from merge requests opened from forks may run in the parent project.

ciConfigPath.description: CI config file path for the project.

ciDefaultGitDepth.description: Default number of revisions for shallow cloning.

ciDeletePipelinesInSeconds.description: Pipelines older than the configured time are deleted.

ciDisplayPipelineVariables.description: Whether pipeline variables are displayed in the UI.

ciForwardDeploymentEnabled.description: When a new deployment job starts, skip older deployment jobs that are still pending.

ciForwardDeploymentRollbackAllowed.description: Allow job retries even if the deployment job is outdated.

ciIdTokenSubClaimComponents.description: Fields included in the sub claim of the ID Token. Accepts an array starting with `projectPath`. The array might also include `refType` and `ref`. Defaults to `["projectPath", "refType", "ref"]`. Introduced in GitLab 17.10.

ciJobTokenScopeEnabled.description: Whether the CI/CD job token access scope is enabled (limits which projects can be accessed using the job token).

ciOptInJwt.description: Whether the project must explicitly opt in to receive ID tokens in CI jobs.

ciPipelineVariablesMinimumOverrideRole.description: The minimum role required to set variables when running pipelines and jobs. Introduced in GitLab 17.1. Valid values are `developer`, `maintainer`, `owner`, `noOneAllowed`.

ciPushRepositoryForJobTokenAllowed.description: Whether pushes to the repository using the CI/CD job token are allowed.

ciRestrictPipelineCancellationRole.description: The role required to cancel a pipeline or job. Premium and Ultimate only. Valid values are `developer`, `maintainer`, `noOne`.

ciSeparatedCaches.description: Use separate caches for protected branches.

complianceFrameworks.description: Compliance frameworks applied to the project. Premium and Ultimate only.

containerExpirationPolicies.description: The image cleanup policy for this project.

containerRegistryAccessLevel.description: Set visibility of container registry for this project. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

containerRegistryEnabled.deprecationMessage: Use `containerRegistryAccessLevel` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
containerRegistryEnabled.description: Whether the container registry is enabled for the project.

containerRegistryImagePrefix.description: The image prefix used by the container registry for this project.

createdAt.description: Creation time for the project.

defaultBranch.description: The default branch name of the project.

emailsDisabled.description: Whether email notifications are disabled for the project.

enforceAuthChecksOnUploads.description: Whether authentication checks are enforced when uploading to the project.

environmentsAccessLevel.description: Set the environments access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

externalAuthorizationClassificationLabel.description: The classification label for the project.

featureFlagsAccessLevel.description: Set the feature flags access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

forkedFromProjects.description: Present if the project is a fork. Contains information about the upstream project.

forkingAccessLevel.description: Set the forking access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

forksCount.description: The number of forks of the project.

groupRunnersEnabled.description: Whether group runners are enabled for the project.

httpUrlToRepo.description: The HTTP clone URL of the project.

id.description: The integer that uniquely identifies the project within the gitlab install.

importError.description: The import error, if it exists, for the project.

importStatus.description: The import status of the project.

importType.description: The type of import used to create the project (for example `github`, `bitbucket`).

importUrl.description: URL the project was imported from.

infrastructureAccessLevel.description: Set the infrastructure access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

issueBranchTemplate.description: Template used to suggest a branch name when creating one from an issue.

issuesAccessLevel.description: Set the issues access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

issuesEnabled.deprecationMessage: Use `issuesAccessLevel` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
issuesEnabled.description: Whether issues are enabled for the project.

issuesTemplate.description: Default description template for new issues.

jobsEnabled.deprecationMessage: Use `buildsAccessLevel` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
jobsEnabled.description: Whether jobs are enabled for the project.

keepLatestArtifact.description: Disable or enable the ability to keep the latest artifact for this project.

lastActivityAt.description: Last activity time for the project.

modelRegistryAccessLevel.description: The visibility of machine learning model registry.

mrDefaultTargetSelf.description: For forks, whether merge requests target the fork itself rather than the upstream project by default.

namespaceId.deprecationMessage: Use `namespace[0].id` instead, to be removed in 20.0.

onlyMirrorProtectedBranches.description: Whether `onlyMirrorProtectedBranches` is enabled for the project.

openIssuesCount.description: The number of open issues for the project.

operationsAccessLevel.description: Set the operations access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

owners.description: The owner of the project. Only populated when the calling token has administrator scope.

packageRegistryAccessLevel.description: The visibility of the package registry.

packagesEnabled.deprecationMessage: Use `packageRegistryAccessLevel` instead, to be removed in 20.0.

pagesAccessLevel.description: Set the GitLab Pages access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

pathWithNamespace.description: The path of the repository with namespace.

preReceiveSecretDetectionEnabled.description: Whether pre-receive secret detection is enabled for the project.

preventMergeWithoutJiraIssue.description: Whether merge requests require an associated issue from Jira. Premium and Ultimate only.

printingMergeRequestLinkEnabled.description: Show link to create/view merge request when pushing from the command line.

protectMergeRequestPipelines.description: Whether pipelines triggered for merge requests run with project secrets and protected variables, instead of the contributor's lower-privileged context.

publicBuilds.deprecationMessage: Use `publicJobs` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
publicBuilds.description: If true, jobs can be viewed by non-project members. Alias for `publicJobs`.

publicJobs.description: If true, jobs can be viewed by non-project members.

pushRules.description: Push rules for the project. Push rules are only available on Enterprise plans and if the authenticated user has permission to read them.

readmeUrl.description: The URL of the project README.

releasesAccessLevel.description: Set the releases access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

removeSourceBranchAfterMerge.description: Enable `Delete source branch` option by default for all new merge requests.

repositoryAccessLevel.description: Set the repository access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

repositoryStorage.description: Which storage shard the repository is on. (administrator only)

requestAccessEnabled.description: Whether requesting access is enabled for the project.

requirementsAccessLevel.description: Set the requirements access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

requirementsEnabled.description: Whether the requirements feature is enabled. Premium and Ultimate only.

resolveOutdatedDiffDiscussions.description: Automatically resolve merge request diffs discussions on lines changed with a push.

resourceGroupDefaultProcessMode.description: The default resource group process mode for the project.

restrictUserDefinedVariables.deprecationMessage: Use `ciPipelineVariablesMinimumOverrideRole` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
restrictUserDefinedVariables.description: Allow only users with the Maintainer role to pass user-defined variables when triggering a pipeline.

runnerTokenExpirationInterval.description: Runner token expiration interval, in seconds.

runnersToken.description: Registration token to use during runner setup.

securityAndComplianceAccessLevel.description: Set the security and compliance access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

securityAndComplianceEnabled.description: Whether the security and compliance feature is enabled.

serviceDeskAddress.description: The Service Desk email address for the project.

serviceDeskEnabled.description: Whether Service Desk is enabled for the project.

sharedRunnersEnabled.description: Whether shared runners are enabled for the project.

sharedWithGroups.description: Describes groups which have access shared to this project.

snippetsAccessLevel.description: Set the snippets access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

snippetsEnabled.deprecationMessage: Use `snippetsAccessLevel` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
snippetsEnabled.description: Whether snippets are enabled for the project.

squashCommitTemplate.description: Template used to create squash commit message in merge requests.

squashOption.description: The project's squash option for merge requests (`never`, `always`, `defaultOn`, `defaultOff`).

sshUrlToRepo.description: The SSH clone URL of the project.

starCount.description: The number of stars on the project.

suggestionCommitMessage.description: The commit message used to apply merge request suggestions.

tagLists.deprecationMessage: Use `topics` instead, to be removed in 20.0.

topics.description: The list of topics for the project.

updatedAt.description: The time the project was last updated.

visibility.description: The visibility of the project (`private`, `internal`, `public`).

visibilityLevel.deprecationMessage: Use `visibility` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
visibilityLevel.description: The visibility of the project. Alias for `visibility`.

webUrl.description: URL that can be used to find the project in a browser.

wikiAccessLevel.description: Set the wiki access level. Valid values are `disabled`, `private`, `enabled`.

wikiEnabled.deprecationMessage: Use `wikiAccessLevel` instead, to be removed in 20.0.
wikiEnabled.description: Whether wiki is enabled for the project.

assigneeIds.description: The IDs of the users to assign the issue to.

closedAt.description: When the issue was closed. Date time string, ISO 8601 formatted, for example 2016-03-11T03:45:40Z.

closedByUserId.description: The ID of the user that closed the issue. Use `gitlab.User` data source to get more information about the user.

confidential.description: Set an issue to be confidential.

createdAt.description: When the issue was created. Date time string, ISO 8601 formatted, for example 2016-03-11T03:45:40Z. Requires administrator or project/group owner rights.

description.description: The description of an issue. Limited to 1,048,576 characters.

discussionLocked.description: Whether the issue is locked for discussions or not.

discussionToResolve.description: The ID of a discussion to resolve. This fills out the issue with a default description and mark the discussion as resolved. Use in combination with merge*request*to*resolve*discussions_of.

downvotes.description: The number of downvotes the issue has received.

dueDate.description: The due date. Date time string in the format YYYY-MM-DD, for example 2016-03-11.

epicId.description: ID of the epic to add the issue to. Valid values are greater than or equal to 0.

epicIssueId.description: The ID of the epic issue.

externalId.description: The external ID of the issue.

humanTimeEstimate.description: The human-readable time estimate of the issue.

humanTotalTimeSpent.description: The human-readable total time spent of the issue.

iid.description: The internal ID of the project's issue.

issueId.description: The instance-wide ID of the issue.

issueLinkId.description: The ID of the issue link.

issueType.description: The type of issue. Valid values are: `issue`, `incident`, `testCase`.

mergeRequestsCount.description: The number of merge requests associated with the issue.

milestoneId.description: The global ID of a milestone to assign issue. To find the milestoneId associated with a milestone, view an issue with the milestone assigned and use the API to retrieve the issue's details.

movedToId.description: The ID of the issue that was moved to.

project.description: The name or ID of the project.

state.description: The state of the issue. Valid values are: `opened`, `closed`.

subscribed.description: Whether the authenticated user is subscribed to the issue or not.

taskCompletionStatuses.description: The task completion status. It's always a one element list.

timeEstimate.description: The time estimate of the issue.

totalTimeSpent.description: The total time spent of the issue.

updatedAt.description: When the issue was updated. Date time string, ISO 8601 formatted, for example 2016-03-11T03:45:40Z.

upvotes.description: The number of upvotes the issue has received.

userNotesCount.description: The number of user notes on the issue.

webUrl.description: The web URL of the issue.

weight.description: The weight of the issue. Valid values are greater than or equal to 0.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getProjectIssues.

assigneeId.description: Return issues assigned to the given user id. Mutually exclusive with assignee_username. None returns unassigned issues. Any returns issues with an assignee.

assigneeUsername.description: Return issues assigned to the given username. Similar to assignee*id and mutually exclusive with assignee*id. In GitLab CE, the assigneeUsername array should only contain a single value. Otherwise, an invalid parameter error is returned.

authorId.description: Return issues created by the given user id. Combine with scope=all or scope=assigned*to*me.

createdAfter.description: Return issues created on or after the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format (2019-03-15T08:00:00Z)

createdBefore.description: Return issues created on or before the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format (2019-03-15T08:00:00Z)

dueDate.description: Return issues that have no due date, are overdue, or whose due date is this week, this month, or between two weeks ago and next month. Accepts: 0 (no due date), any, today, tomorrow, overdue, week, month, next*month*and*previous*two_weeks.

iids.description: Return only the issues having the given iid

issueType.description: Filter to a given type of issue. Valid values are [issue incident testCase].

labels.description: Return issues with labels. Issues must have all labels to be returned. None lists all issues with no labels. Any lists all issues with at least one label. No+Label (Deprecated) lists all issues with no labels. Predefined names are case-insensitive.

milestone.description: The milestone title. None lists all issues with no milestone. Any lists all issues that have an assigned milestone.

myReactionEmoji.description: Return issues reacted by the authenticated user by the given emoji. None returns issues not given a reaction. Any returns issues given at least one reaction.

notAssigneeId.description: Return issues that do not match the assignee id.

notAuthorId.description: Return issues that do not match the author id.

notLabels.description: Return issues that do not match the labels.

notMilestone.description: Return issues that do not match the milestone.

notMyReactionEmoji.description: Return issues not reacted by the authenticated user by the given emoji.

orderBy.description: Return issues ordered by. Valid values are `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `priority`, `dueDate`, `relativePosition`, `labelPriority`, `milestoneDue`, `popularity`, `weight`. Default is created_at

project.description: The name or id of the project.

scope.description: Return issues for the given scope. Valid values are `createdByMe`, `assignedToMe`, `all`. Defaults to all.

search.description: Search project issues against their title and description

sort.description: Return issues sorted in asc or desc order. Default is desc

updatedAfter.description: Return issues updated on or after the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format (2019-03-15T08:00:00Z)

updatedBefore.description: Return issues updated on or before the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format (2019-03-15T08:00:00Z)

weight.description: Return issues with the specified weight. None returns issues with no weight assigned. Any returns issues with a weight assigned.

withLabelsDetails.description: If true, the response returns more details for each label in labels field: :name, :color, :description, :description*html, :text*color. Default is false.

authorId.description: Return merge requests created by the given user ID.

createdBefore.description: Return merge requests created before the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

iids.description: The unique internal IDs of the merge requests.

myReactionEmoji.description: Return merge requests reacted to by the authenticated user with the given emoji. `None` returns issues not given a reaction. `Any` returns issues given at least one reaction.

orderBy.description: Return requests ordered by `createdAt`, `title` or `updatedAt`. Default is `createdAt`.

reviewerUsername.description: Return merge requests reviewed by the given username. `None` returns merge requests with no reviews. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer.

search.description: Search merge requests against their `title` or `description`.

sort.description: Return requests sorted in `asc` or `desc` order. Default is `desc`.

updatedAfter.description: Return merge requests updated after the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

updatedBefore.description: Return merge requests updated before the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

wip.description: Filter merge requests against their wip status. `yes` to return only draft merge requests, `no` to return non-draft merge requests.

authorId.description: Return merge requests created by the given user ID.

authorUsername.description: Return merge requests created by the given username.

createdAfter.description: Return merge requests created after the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

createdBefore.description: Return merge requests created before the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

iids.description: The unique internal IDs of the merge requests.

milestone.description: Return only merge requests for a specific milestone. `None` returns merge requests with no milestone. `Any` returns merge requests that have an assigned milestone.

myReactionEmoji.description: Return merge requests reacted to by the authenticated user with the given emoji. `None` returns issues not given a reaction. `Any` returns issues given at least one reaction.

orderBy.description: Return requests ordered by `createdAt`, `title` or `updatedAt`. Default is `createdAt`.

project.description: The ID or path of the project.

reviewerUsername.description: Return merge requests reviewed by the given username. `None` returns merge requests with no reviews. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer.

scope.description: Return merge requests for the given scope: `createdByMe`, `assignedToMe`, or `all`.

search.description: Search merge requests against their `title` or `description`.

sort.description: Return requests sorted in `asc` or `desc` order. Default is `desc`.

sourceBranch.description: Return merge requests with the given source branch.

state.description: Return all merge requests (all) or just those that are opened, closed, locked, or merged.

targetBranch.description: Return merge requests with the given target branch.

updatedAfter.description: Return merge requests updated after the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

updatedBefore.description: Return merge requests updated before the given time. Expected in RFC3339 format (2006-01-02T15:04:05Z).

wip.description: Filter merge requests against their wip status. `yes` to return only draft merge requests, `no` to return non-draft merge requests.

createdAt.description: The time of creation of the milestone. Date time string, ISO 8601 formatted, for example 2016-03-11T03:45:40Z.

dueDate.description: The due date of the milestone. Date time string in the format YYYY-MM-DD, for example 2016-03-11.

id.description: The ID of this datasource. In the format `<project:milestone-id>`.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getProjects.

active.description: Limit by projects that are not archived and not marked for deletion. If `false`, return only projects that are archived or marked for deletion.

groupId.description: The ID of the group owned by the authenticated user to look projects for within. Cannot be used with `minAccessLevel`, `withProgrammingLanguage` or `statistics`.

includeSubgroups.description: Include projects in subgroups of this group. Default is `false`. Needs `groupId`.

maxQueryablePages.description: The maximum number of project results pages that may be queried. Prevents overloading your Gitlab instance in case of a misconfiguration (default 10).

membership.description: Limit by projects that the current user is a member of.

minAccessLevel.description: Limit to projects where current user has at least this access level, refer to the [official documentation](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/permissions/#default-roles) for values. Cannot be used with `groupId`.

orderBy.description: Return projects ordered ordered by: `id`, `name`, `path`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `lastActivityAt`, `similarity`, `repositorySize`, `storageSize`, `packagesSize`, `wikiSize`. Some values or only available in certain circumstances. See [upstream docs](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/projects/#list-all-projects) for details.

owned.description: Limit by projects owned by the current user.

page.description: The first page to begin the query on (default 1).

perPage.description: The number of results to return per page (default 20, maximum 100).

search.description: Return list of authorized projects matching the search criteria.

simple.description: Return only the ID, URL, name, and path of each project.

sort.description: Return projects sorted in `asc` or `desc` order. Default is `desc`.

starred.description: Limit by projects starred by the current user.

statistics.description: Include project statistics. Cannot be used with `groupId`.

topics.description: Limit by projects that have all of the given topics.

visibility.description: Limit by visibility `public`, `internal`, or `private`.

withCustomAttributes.description: Include custom attributes in response *(admins only)*.

withIssuesEnabled.description: Limit by projects with issues feature enabled. Default is `false`.

withMergeRequestsEnabled.description: Limit by projects with merge requests feature enabled. Default is `false`.

withProgrammingLanguage.description: Limit by projects which use the given programming language. Cannot be used with `groupId`.

withShared.description: Include projects shared to this group. Default is `true`. Needs `groupId`.

id.description: The ID of this datasource. In the format of a hash taken from the search options.

orderBy.description: Order the users' list by `id`, `name`, `username`, `createdAt` or `updatedAt`. (Requires administrator privileges)

search.description: Search users by username, name or email.

sort.description: Sort users' list in asc or desc order. (Requires administrator privileges)

username.description: Get a single user with a specific username.

pulumi:providers:gitlab/terraformConfig.description: This function returns a Terraform config object with terraform-namecased keys,to be used with the Terraform Module Provider.
